states.name: IdleOff
states.description: No level transition is in progress, device output is off, RemainingTime is zero. CurrentLevel is stable but may be any value within allowed range. OnOff cluster OnOff attribute is FALSE.

states.name: IdleOn
states.description: No level transition is in progress, device output is on, RemainingTime is zero. CurrentLevel is stable and within the allowed range. OnOff cluster OnOff attribute is TRUE.

states.name: LevelTransitionOff
states.description: A level transition is in progress while the device output remains off. RemainingTime is greater than zero and is counting down until transition completion. This includes transitions started by base commands when ExecuteIfOff is TRUE, and WithOnOff commands that intentionally leave OnOff FALSE while off.

states.name: LevelTransitionOnBase
states.description: A level transition initiated by a base Level Control command (MoveToLevel, Move, Step) is in progress while the device output is on. RemainingTime is greater than zero and counting down until completion. Base commands do not couple reaching minimum level to OnOff changes.

states.name: LevelTransitionOnWithOnOff
states.description: A level transition initiated by a WithOnOff variant (MoveToLevelWithOnOff, MoveWithOnOff, StepWithOnOff) is in progress while the device output is on. RemainingTime is greater than zero and counting down until completion. For these transitions, reaching the device minimum level may cause OnOff to become FALSE.

transitions.timing_requirements: Base MoveToLevel is ignored with respect to level and on/off changes when ExecuteIfOff is 0 and device is off; response is still sent at application level.
RemainingTime represents duration in units of 0.1 seconds; device may shorten if variable rate not supported.
RemainingTime represents duration in units of 0.1 seconds; device may shorten if variable rate not supported.
Timer tick represents decrement of RemainingTime by 1 unit (0.1 seconds) for base-command transitions while on.
Timer tick represents decrement of RemainingTime by 1 unit (0.1 seconds) for WithOnOff-command transitions while on.
Timer tick represents decrement of RemainingTime by 1 unit (0.1 seconds) while off.
Base-command transition completes when RemainingTime reaches zero; device remains on even if level is at the minimum.
WithOnOff transition completes above minimum level; device remains on.
WithOnOff transition completes at minimum level; device turns off in accordance with the Level Control On/Off coupling rules.
Off-state transition completes when RemainingTime reaches zero; device remains off.
Command is rejected at application level with INVALID_COMMAND when Rate is 0; no state change is modeled.
Continuous movement proceeds at EffectiveRate until reaching device limit; RemainingTime models time until limit while off.
Continuous movement from a base Move command proceeds at EffectiveRate until reaching device limit; RemainingTime models time until limit while on.
Command is rejected at application level with INVALID_COMMAND when StepSize is 0; no state change is modeled.
Base Step is ignored with respect to level and on/off changes when ExecuteIfOff is 0 and device is off.

actions: TargetLevel := compute_step_target(CurrentLevel, StepMode, StepSize, MinLevel, MaxLevel)
transitions.timing_requirements: RemainingTime represents scaled transition time based on actual delta relative to requested StepSize when clamped, while off.

actions: TargetLevel := compute_step_target(CurrentLevel, StepMode, StepSize, MinLevel, MaxLevel)
transitions.timing_requirements: RemainingTime represents scaled transition time based on actual delta relative to requested StepSize when clamped, for base Step while on.
A new base Move command while on overrides existing base movement parameters and RemainingTime.
A new base Move command while off overrides existing movement parameters and RemainingTime when ExecuteIfOff allows execution.
Stop when no transition is in progress has no effect on level or RemainingTime.
Stop when no transition is in progress has no effect on level or RemainingTime.
Stop terminates any in-progress base transition immediately while keeping the device on.
Stop terminates any in-progress WithOnOff transition immediately while keeping the device on at its then-current level.
Stop terminates any in-progress transition immediately while keeping the device off.
When device is off, MoveToLevelWithOnOff turns device on and begins transition to TargetLevel, independent of ExecuteIfOff, matching the lighting state change table.
When already on, MoveToLevelWithOnOff behaves like MoveToLevel but with On/Off coupling at completion when at minimum.
Existing off transition is overridden; WithOnOff variant turns device on and continues transition while on with On/Off coupling.
MoveWithOnOff with Rate 0 is invalid and causes INVALID_COMMAND at application level; no state change is modeled.
When device is off, MoveWithOnOff up turns device on and moves level toward maximum, independent of ExecuteIfOff, matching the lighting state change table semantics for turning on.
When device is off and MoveWithOnOff down is issued, CurrentLevel moves toward minimum while keeping OnOff FALSE, as in the lighting state change table where the device remains off at minimum.
When on, MoveWithOnOff up raises level to maximum and keeps the device on; OnOff remains TRUE even at full brightness.
When on, MoveWithOnOff down lowers level towards minimum; if the resulting level is minimum at completion, OnOff becomes FALSE per the LevelTransitionOnWithOnOff completion rules.

actions: TargetLevel := compute_step_target(CurrentLevel, StepMode, StepSize, MinLevel, MaxLevel)
transitions.timing_requirements: When device is on, StepWithOnOff behaves like Step, but OnOff may be updated at transition completion depending on whether the final level equals the device minimum.
StepWithOnOff with StepSize 0 is invalid and causes INVALID_COMMAND at application level; no state change is modeled.

actions: TargetLevel := compute_step_target(CurrentLevel, StepMode, StepSize, MinLevel, MaxLevel)
transitions.timing_requirements: When device is off, StepWithOnOff updates CurrentLevel over time but keeps OnOff FALSE, consistent with lighting table entries where device remains off.
Completion of a StepWithOnOff or MoveWithOnOff transition while Off leaves OnOff FALSE and finalizes CurrentLevel at its target.
StopWithOnOff has identical behavior to Stop for level movement while off.
StopWithOnOff has identical behavior to Stop for base-command level movement while on; OnOff remains in its current value when stopping early.
StopWithOnOff has identical behavior to Stop for WithOnOff-command level movement while on; OnOff remains unchanged at the time of stopping.
StopWithOnOff when idle has no effect on level or RemainingTime while off.
StopWithOnOff when idle has no effect on level or RemainingTime while on.
Immediate change of frequency without timing behavior while off.
Immediate change of frequency without timing behavior while on.

scene_behaviors: When a scene is stored via the Scenes Management server cluster, the Level Control attributes CurrentLevel and CurrentFrequency (if the Frequency feature is supported) are saved in the scene table extension field set for the endpoint.

On scene recall, the stored CurrentLevel and CurrentFrequency values are restored atomically, which may result in a logical transition from IdleOn to IdleOn or IdleOff to IdleOff with updated attribute values without invoking Move, Step, or MoveToLevel commands.
Unsupported Level Control attributes for a given feature configuration are still present in the scene extension field set but are ignored on recall, preserving interoperability.

definitions.definition: The effective minimum level that the device can represent at its output. In the lighting feature, this typically corresponds to the MinLevel attribute value, which represents the lowest allowed non-zero brightness value. When CurrentLevel equals the device minimum, the device is considered at minimal light output.
definitions.usage_context: Used conceptually in transitions that clamp levels or decide when OnOff should become FALSE for WithOnOff operations at minimum brightness.

definitions.definition: The effective maximum level that the device can represent at its output. This is usually the MaxLevel attribute value if implemented, or the highest supported value within the allowed range when MaxLevel is not present. It represents the maximum brightness or output level.
definitions.usage_context: Used for Move and MoveWithOnOff commands moving up until the maximum level is reached.

definitions.definition: A boolean interpretation of the ExecuteIfOff bit inside the temporary options bitmap. When this bit is TRUE, base Level Control commands such as MoveToLevel, Move, and Step execute their level-changing effects even when the OnOff attribute is FALSE. When FALSE, these base commands are inhibited while the device is off. WithOnOff variants are defined to operate regardless of this bit when interpreting the lighting state change table.
definitions.usage_context: Appears in guard conditions determining whether a base command is allowed to modify CurrentLevel in the IdleOff and LevelTransitionOff states.

definitions.definition: A temporary bitmap stored as a conceptual internal attribute derived from the persistent Options attribute and the OptionsMask and OptionsOverride fields included in a command. For each bit position where OptionsMask has value 1, the corresponding bit from OptionsOverride is used; otherwise, the bit from Options is used. This bitmap influences whether base commands execute when off and whether color temperature is coupled to level changes.
definitions.usage_context: Computed logically at the beginning of command handling and then interpreted by helper functions such as ExecuteIfOff(tempOptions).

definitions.definition: An atomic action that updates the internal tempOptions bitmap by combining the stored Options attribute with OptionsMask and OptionsOverride command fields. It encapsulates the per-bit selection of either the existing Options value or the overriding value indicated by OptionsOverride, without exposing control flow.
definitions.usage_context: Referenced in actions for all commands that include OptionsMask and OptionsOverride fields, ensuring consistent application of option overrides before any other per-command logic uses tempOptions.

definitions.definition: An abstract action that denotes the absence of any modification to the modeled attributes or state variables. It is used where the protocol mandates that a command has no effect beyond responses, which are not represented in this FSM.
definitions.usage_context: Used in transitions for invalid parameters such as Rate == 0 or StepSize == 0, and for ignored commands when ExecuteIfOff is FALSE.

definitions.definition: The effective duration for a level transition in units of tenths of a second. It is obtained from the TransitionTime command field when present and non-null, or from the OnOffTransitionTime attribute when that field is null, or from an implementation-defined minimal duration when neither timing source applies. Devices without variable rate support may treat this duration as a minimal practical time rather than a precise value.
definitions.usage_context: Used to set RemainingTime when handling MoveToLevel and MoveToLevelWithOnOff commands.

definitions.definition: The effective rate of level change in units per second for continuous Move and MoveWithOnOff commands. This rate is determined from the Rate command parameter when non-null and non-zero, from the DefaultMoveRate attribute when the Rate parameter is null and DefaultMoveRate is present and non-null, or from a device-specific maximum practical rate when neither source applies. Devices may ignore detailed rate control when variable rate support is absent.
definitions.usage_context: Used to parameterize continuous level movement in Move and MoveWithOnOff transitions.

definitions.definition: The effective duration of a Step or StepWithOnOff transition based on the requested TransitionTime, the actual difference between the starting level and the computed target level, and the requested StepSize. If the target level is clamped to the device minimum or maximum, the duration is proportionally reduced compared to the base duration derived from the command field.
definitions.usage_context: Used to set RemainingTime when executing Step and StepWithOnOff commands, ensuring timing reflects any clamping behavior.

definitions.definition: The target level calculated for a single step operation, based on the current level, step direction, step magnitude, and device limits. For upward steps, the magnitude is added to the current level and then restricted to be no greater than the device maximum. For downward steps, the magnitude is subtracted from the current level and then restricted not to fall below the device minimum.
definitions.usage_context: Determines TargetLevel for Step and StepWithOnOff transitions before duration is computed.

definitions.definition: A conceptual mapping from a requested level value to a legal level within the range [MinLevel, MaxLevel]. Values lower than MinLevel become MinLevel and values higher than MaxLevel become MaxLevel. This prevents attributes from assuming out-of-range values.
definitions.usage_context: Used whenever commands specify a target level, ensuring state invariants on CurrentLevel are preserved.

definitions.definition: The logical initiation of a time-based change from a starting level to a target level over a specified RemainingTime. During the transition, CurrentLevel changes progressively until it reaches the target value at RemainingTime equal to zero.
definitions.usage_context: Used by MoveToLevel, Step, MoveToLevelWithOnOff, and StepWithOnOff actions to begin transitions and set RemainingTime.

definitions.definition: The function describing how CurrentLevel evolves between the starting and target values during an ongoing time-based level transition. It ensures that CurrentLevel moves monotonically in the correct direction and will reach the intended final level when RemainingTime becomes zero.
definitions.usage_context: Used on each internal_timer_tick while RemainingTime remains greater than one for LevelTransitionOff, LevelTransitionOnBase, and LevelTransitionOnWithOnOff states.

definitions.definition: The level value at the moment when a transition finishes, representing the command’s intended target as possibly adjusted by device limits or timing behavior. It is used to set CurrentLevel when RemainingTime becomes zero.
definitions.usage_context: Applied on the last timer tick of a transition to guarantee that the final level equals the planned TargetLevel.

definitions.definition: The logical configuration of a continuous movement process for the Move and MoveWithOnOff commands, including direction and rate. It continues to adjust the level until a hard limit such as minimum or maximum is reached or until stopped.
definitions.usage_context: Used at the beginning of continuous move commands to configure internal movement behavior and RemainingTime estimates.

definitions.definition: An estimated transition time for a continuous move, based on the requested or derived rate and the distance to the relevant device limit in the chosen direction. This estimate populates RemainingTime so that the state model can represent the duration of movement until the limit is reached.
definitions.usage_context: Used with Move and MoveWithOnOff commands to represent timing of level movement in the FSM.

definitions.definition: The immediate cessation of any ongoing level transition, leaving CurrentLevel at its value at the time termination occurs and setting RemainingTime to zero.
definitions.usage_context: Used in Stop and StopWithOnOff transitions to abort movement and enter an idle state.

definitions.definition: A device-specific mapping from a requested frequency to the nearest frequency value that the device can generate within its MinFrequency and MaxFrequency range. If no sufficiently close approximation exists according to manufacturer rules, the mapping yields no usable result.
definitions.usage_context: Used by MoveToClosestFrequency transitions to compute the new CurrentFrequency or to indicate that a CONSTRAINT_ERROR should be returned by higher layers.

definitions.definition: A predicate that indicates whether the effective final level of a transition has reached the device’s minimum level. It is used when determining whether a transition with On/Off coupling should cause the device to turn off at the end of movement.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guard conditions for internal_timer_tick transitions in the LevelTransitionOnWithOnOff state that might change OnOff from TRUE to FALSE on completion.

definitions.definition: A boolean interpretation of the Frequency feature bit in the FeatureMap attribute. When TRUE, frequency-related attributes such as CurrentFrequency, MinFrequency, and MaxFrequency are supported and commands like MoveToClosestFrequency are valid.
definitions.usage_context: Appears in guards for MoveToClosestFrequency transitions to ensure that the command only affects state when the frequency feature is implemented.

references.name: On/Off Cluster
references.description: Supplies the OnOff attribute used to distinguish IdleOn and IdleOff states and to apply On/Off coupling for the WithOnOff command variants.

references.name: Color Control Cluster
references.description: Optionally coupled to level changes through the CoupleColorTempToLevel bit in the Options attribute; not explicitly modeled in state transitions but relevant to tempOptions semantics.

references.name: Scenes Management server cluster
references.description: Stores and recalls Level Control attributes such as CurrentLevel and CurrentFrequency in scene table extension field sets, influencing scene_behaviors.

references.name: FeatureMap attribute
references.description: Determines which Level Control features (OnOff, Lighting, Frequency) are supported, controlling the availability of attributes and commands like MoveToClosestFrequency.

references.name: ClusterRevision attribute
references.description: Indicates the revision of the Level Control cluster implemented; used to ensure the correct behavioral expectations for this FSM.

security_properties.description: Verifies that CurrentLevel never leaves the allowed range defined by MinLevel and MaxLevel in any reachable state or during any transition, preventing corrupted or invalid brightness values.
security_properties.formal_specification: forall trace, step. state_at(step).CurrentLevel != null -> (state_at(step).CurrentLevel >= state_at(step).MinLevel && state_at(step).CurrentLevel <= state_at(step).MaxLevel)
security_properties.attack_vector: An attacker might attempt to send malformed commands with out-of-range Level or StepSize parameters hoping to force the device into an invalid level configuration.
security_properties.mitigation: The FSM uses clamping and step-target computation and models invariants that always constrain CurrentLevel within [MinLevel, MaxLevel].

security_properties.description: Ensures that when ExecuteIfOff is FALSE and the device is off, base level-changing commands (MoveToLevel, Move, Step) do not change CurrentLevel or turn the device on, while WithOnOff variants retain their specified ability to act when off.
security_properties.formal_specification: forall trace, step, cmd. cmd in {MoveToLevel, Move, Step} && state_at(step).OnOff == FALSE && ExecuteIfOff(tempOptions_at(step, cmd)) == FALSE -> state_at(step+1).CurrentLevel == state_at(step).CurrentLevel && state_at(step+1).OnOff == FALSE
security_properties.attack_vector: A malicious controller might try to bypass user expectations by issuing base commands to alter the level while the device is off, despite ExecuteIfOff being cleared.
security_properties.mitigation: Guard conditions in the FSM prevent level and OnOff changes for base commands when OnOff is FALSE and ExecuteIfOff is FALSE, while modeling WithOnOff commands according to the normative state change table. The introduction of tempOptions as an explicit attribute and the use of build_temp_options_action ensure ExecuteIfOff decisions are based on the per-command temporary bitmap.

security_properties.description: Checks that reaching the device minimum level only causes OnOff to become FALSE when the transition was initiated by a WithOnOff variant, and that base commands never change OnOff at minimum level.
security_properties.formal_specification: forall trace, step. transition(step).from_state == LevelTransitionOnBase && transition(step).trigger == internal_timer_tick && state_at(step).RemainingTime == 1 -> state_at(step+1).OnOff == state_at(step).OnOff; forall trace, step. transition(step).from_state == LevelTransitionOnWithOnOff && transition(step).trigger == internal_timer_tick && state_at(step).RemainingTime == 1 && is_min_level_reached_at(step) == TRUE -> state_at(step+1).OnOff == FALSE
security_properties.attack_vector: An attacker might craft sequences of base commands that force the level to minimum in an attempt to spuriously switch the device off by exploiting mis-implemented coupling.
security_properties.mitigation: The FSM separates base-command transitions (LevelTransitionOnBase) from WithOnOff transitions (LevelTransitionOnWithOnOff) and only applies OnOff := FALSE at minimum in the latter, reflecting the specification and lighting state change table.

security_properties.description: Ensures that CurrentFrequency is only modified by MoveToClosestFrequency when the Frequency feature is present and that it always remains within supported bounds, or else the command is rejected at higher layers.
security_properties.formal_specification: forall trace, step. transition(step).trigger == MoveToClosestFrequency && FeatureFQ_at(step) == TRUE && select_closest_supported_frequency_at(step) != null -> (state_at(step+1).CurrentFrequency >= state_at(step+1).MinFrequency && state_at(step+1).CurrentFrequency <= state_at(step+1).MaxFrequency)
security_properties.attack_vector: An attacker could send arbitrary frequency values attempting to drive the device beyond its physical capabilities or crash its control logic.
security_properties.mitigation: The FSM models selection of a closest supported frequency and only updates CurrentFrequency when a valid value exists, otherwise higher logic returns CONSTRAINT_ERROR.

security_properties.description: Verifies that whenever RemainingTime is greater than zero, either internal timer ticks reduce it to zero or a Stop or StopWithOnOff command sets it to zero, preventing indefinitely stuck transitions.
security_properties.formal_specification: forall trace, step. state_at(step).RemainingTime > 0 -> exists step2 >= step. state_at(step2).RemainingTime == 0
security_properties.attack_vector: By interfering with timer events or repeatedly sending commands, an attacker might try to keep the device locked in a transition state, affecting responsiveness and battery consumption.
security_properties.mitigation: The FSM includes timer_tick transitions that strictly decrease RemainingTime and Stop and StopWithOnOff transitions that force it to zero, guaranteeing eventual return to an idle state.

security_properties.description: Ensures that invalid command parameters such as Rate == 0 for Move and MoveWithOnOff or StepSize == 0 for Step and StepWithOnOff do not alter the device state, thereby preventing invalid movements.
security_properties.formal_specification: forall trace, step. transition(step).trigger in {Move, MoveWithOnOff} && Rate(step) == 0 -> state_at(step+1) == state_at(step); forall trace, step. transition(step).trigger in {Step, StepWithOnOff} && StepSize(step) == 0 -> state_at(step+1) == state_at(step)
security_properties.attack_vector: A malicious client could send malformed or boundary commands to try to place the device into unforeseen states by exploiting undefined parameter values.
security_properties.mitigation: The FSM models transitions with guards for invalid parameters that yield no state change, mirroring specification requirements for INVALID_COMMAND responses.
